questions.question: Explain the difference between the Collection and Collections classes in Java.
expectedPoints: Collection is an interface that represents a group of objects
Collections is a utility class that contains static methods for operating on collections
Collection defines the contract for collection types
Collections provides algorithms like sort(), binarySearch(), and synchronization wrappers

questions.question: Describe the hierarchy of collection interfaces and explain the purpose of each major interface with examples.
expectedPoints: Collection is the root interface defining basic operations
List extends Collection for ordered sequences with duplicates
Set extends Collection for collections without duplicates
Queue extends Collection for FIFO processing
Deque extends Queue for double-ended queue operations
Each interface has specific implementations with different characteristics
Examples: ArrayList implements List, HashSet implements Set, LinkedList implements both List and Deque
